title: papaver argemone subsp. micranthum
intro: Papaver argemone subsp. micranthum, also known as the small-flowered poppy or small-flowered argemone, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating conditions such as fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Papaver argemone subsp. micranthum include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and providing relief from insomnia and other sleep disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
